track_listing.details: with Roy Harper; Top Gear session 1969-06-02

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Well hello there, Thomas the Vance here, TV on the radio, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.

Tonight we have the first ever session from the Tigers of Pan Tang, and out of the archives, circa 1967 and 1969, we feature a band who were once banned from the Royal Albert Hall, and they were called The Nice.
We've got classic cuts by Lone Star, Neil Young, Wings, Peter Green, Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers, among others,
and new stuff by the Scorpions, Trespass, 10cc, Black Sabbath and Triumph.
Not forgetting our Friday night connection.
But this evening we start with Triumph.
They're doing so well, and deservedly so.
Their album's gone straight into the album charts at number 10, and their single, which we play just there,
Wheels of Steel by Saxon, is at number 37.
And pretty soon they start their own headlining tour. Good luck to them.

And I played that especially for a lady by the name of Sam, with lots and lots of best wishes from Simon, and also Remon, I think it is, and Hi-Fi Honky, and all the staff at Mob, and everybody at Stafford College.
Not forgetting all the friends of Sid, who posted this postcard in Newcastle, friends in Fife, I think it is, and in Glasgow University.
And is it Horse Lethal House up there, including Marjorie and Pat and Bobby and Arthur and Graham, who's somewhere out there in the North Sea.
